items.headline: As AI Moves from Training to Inference, Optics Moves Closer to the Chip
items.note: Imec researchers argue that co-packaged optics will not be enough for future AI systems, pushing the industry toward 2.5D and 3D optical I/O.

items.headline: ANN-Based Lighting Control Cuts Energy Use in Intelligent Buildings
items.note: A real-world smart office study shows how neural network lighting control can reduce energy use and support adaptive building automation.

items.headline: Qualcomm retreats from small cells with eye on bigger 6G prize

items.headline: AI-Driven Embedded Vision: The Future of Drones
items.note: Embedded cameras empower autonomous drones to capture sharp, real-time data for precise, edge-led flight decisions.

items.headline: Korean AI Startup, Furiosa AI, Is Doubling Its Chip Production To 50,000 Units Next Year While Its Upcoming 2nm “Stork” Chip Challenges NVIDIA With The “World’s Best Inference”
